_source: ASHRAE Fundamentals, HVAC troubleshooting guides, EPA 608 certification materials
_note: Long-tail HVAC data. System diagnostics, refrigeration cycle, psychrometrics, load calculations.

four_components.compressor: Pumps refrigerant; raises pressure and temperature; heart of system
four_components.condenser: Outdoor coil; refrigerant rejects heat to outside air; gas → liquid
four_components.metering_device: TXV or piston; restricts flow; causes pressure/temp drop
four_components.evaporator: Indoor coil; refrigerant absorbs heat from indoor air; liquid → gas

r410a_common_pressures.subcool_target: 10-15°F below saturation temp at condenser outlet
r410a_common_pressures.superheat_target: 8-12°F above saturation temp at evaporator outlet (TXV); 10-20°F (piston)

r22_legacy_pressures.note: R22 production banned; retrofit to R410A requires new system (incompatible pressures)

superheat_and_subcool.superheat_definition: Temperature above boiling point; ensures no liquid to compressor
superheat_and_subcool.superheat_measurement: Measure suction line temp; measure suction pressure; convert pressure to temp (PT chart); superheat = actual temp - saturation temp

superheat_diagnosis.high_superheat: >15°F indicates undercharge or restricted metering device
superheat_diagnosis.low_superheat: <5°F indicates overcharge or TXV stuck open; risk of slugging compressor

superheat_and_subcool.subcool_definition: Temperature below condensing point; ensures liquid to metering device
superheat_and_subcool.subcool_measurement: Measure liquid line temp; measure liquid pressure; convert to temp; subcool = saturation temp - actual temp

subcool_diagnosis.low_subcool: <8°F indicates undercharge or restricted liquid line

compressor_not_running.check_thermostat: Set to cool; temp below room temp; display on
compressor_not_running.check_24v: R to C should read 24VAC; if not, transformer blown or breaker off
compressor_not_running.check_contactor: 24V to contactor coil; should pull in; if not, bad coil or low voltage
compressor_not_running.check_compressor: If contactor closed but compressor silent, check capacitor and windings

compressor_running_no_cool.check_charge: Measure superheat/subcool; most common issue is low charge from leak
compressor_running_no_cool.check_airflow: Dirty filter, frozen coil, bad blower; 400 CFM per ton required
compressor_running_no_cool.reversing_valve_stuck: Heat pump only; valve stuck in wrong position; replace valve
compressor_running_no_cool.compressor_failed: Running but not pumping; low amp draw; no pressure differential; replace compressor

short_cycling.dirty_filter: Airflow restricted; evaporator freezes; pressure switch trips; wait 4 hours to thaw
short_cycling.low_charge: Low pressure cutout trips; runs 2-5 minutes then stops; add charge, find leak
short_cycling.oversized_unit: Cools space too quickly; humidity remains high; replace with smaller unit or multi-stage
short_cycling.thermostat_location: In direct sun, near vent, or draft; false readings; relocate thermostat

high_electric_bill.low_charge: Compressor runs longer to achieve setpoint; +30% energy use
high_electric_bill.dirty_coils: Reduced heat transfer; outdoor coil dirty; clean with coil cleaner and hose
high_electric_bill.oversized_ductwork_leaks: 30% loss typical in old systems; seal with mastic (not duct tape)
high_electric_bill.old_inefficient_unit: SEER 8 vs SEER 16 = 50% energy savings; upgrade if >15 years old

frozen_evaporator_coil.diagnosis: Remove filter and panel; ice on coil; measure airflow and superheat
frozen_evaporator_coil.immediate_action: Turn off AC; turn fan to ON; wait 4+ hours for ice to melt; do not run until cause corrected
frozen_evaporator_coil.prevention: Change filter monthly; maintain proper charge; ensure minimum 400 CFM per ton

cfm_requirements.rule_of_thumb: 400 CFM per ton of cooling; 12,000 BTU = 1 ton
cfm_requirements.example: 3-ton system needs 1200 CFM; verify with Magnehelic and flow hood
cfm_requirements.low_airflow_effects: Frozen coil, high superheat, reduced capacity, compressor overheating

duct_sizing.velocity_method: Trunk ducts: 600-900 FPM; branch ducts: 400-600 FPM; too high = noise
duct_sizing.friction_loss_method: 0.08" to 0.1" water column per 100ft for residential
duct_sizing.oversized_ducts: Low velocity; poor throw; stratification; noise at registers
duct_sizing.undersized_ducts: High velocity; noise; increased static pressure; blower works harder

static_pressure_testing.measure_total_external_static: Magnehelic across supply and return plenums; should be <0.5" WC
static_pressure_testing.high_static_effects: Reduced airflow, frozen coil, blower overheating, increased energy use
static_pressure_testing.corrective_action: Open dampers, upsize ducts, use lower MERV filter, check for crushed flex duct

duct_leakage.testing: Duct blaster; pressurize to 25 Pa; measure CFM leakage
duct_leakage.sealing: Mastic (wet sealant) superior to tape; seal all joints, boots, plenums

heating_mode_operation.reversing_valve: Energized in cooling (most common); de-energized in heating; reverses refrigerant flow
heating_mode_operation.defrost_cycle: Outdoor coil ices in heating mode <40°F; reversing valve switches to cooling for 5-10 min to melt ice
heating_mode_operation.auxiliary_heat: Electric resistance strips; activate when outdoor temp <balance point (~30°F) or during defrost
heating_mode_operation.balance_point: Outdoor temp where heat pump capacity = building heat loss; varies by climate and insulation

cop_and_hspf.cop: Coefficient of Performance; heat output / energy input; COP 3.0 = 300% efficient
cop_and_hspf.hspf: Heating Seasonal Performance Factor; 8.2 minimum; 10+ high efficiency
cop_and_hspf.eer_seer: EER = steady-state cooling; SEER = seasonal average; SEER 16 = good; 20+ = excellent

cold_weather_performance.capacity_loss: Heat pump loses 50% capacity at 0°F vs 47°F; aux heat needed
cold_weather_performance.low_temp_compressor: Inverter compressor + vapor injection operates to -15°F
cold_weather_performance.backup_heat: Dual-fuel system switches to gas furnace below setpoint (e.g., 35°F)

sensible_vs_latent.latent_heat: Changes moisture; phase change (evaporation/condensation); measured by humidity
sensible_vs_latent.sensible_heat_ratio: SHR = sensible / total; 0.7-0.8 typical residential; <0.7 = high humidity removal

relative_humidity.definition: Actual moisture / maximum moisture at given temp × 100%
relative_humidity.comfort_range: 40-60% RH; <30% dry skin, static; >60% mold growth
relative_humidity.dew_point: Temp at which air saturates; condensation forms; 65°F dew point = muggy

enthalpy.definition: Total heat content; sensible + latent; measured in BTU/lb
enthalpy.use: Calculating cooling load; comparing indoor vs outdoor air for economizer control

manual_j.purpose: ACCA Manual J; room-by-room heat gain/loss calculation; proper sizing
manual_j.output: Heating and cooling BTU/hr for each room and whole house

rules_of_thumb_are_wrong.bad_rule: 1 ton per 500 sq ft or 600 sq ft
rules_of_thumb_are_wrong.why_wrong: Ignores insulation, windows, orientation, climate, ceiling height
rules_of_thumb_are_wrong.consequences: Oversizing: short cycling, humidity, inefficiency; undersizing: inadequate comfort
rules_of_thumb_are_wrong.correct_method: Manual J calculation; can range from 1 ton per 400 sq ft (leaky old house) to 1 ton per 1200 sq ft (tight modern)

equipment_selection.manual_s: Equipment selection; match capacity to Manual J load; allow <15% oversizing
equipment_selection.manual_d: Duct design; size ducts for calculated airflow; minimize friction loss
equipment_selection.manual_t: Air distribution; register placement and sizing for proper throw and mixing

epa_608_certification.required: Federal law; handle refrigerants only if certified

recovery_requirements.prohibited: Venting refrigerant to atmosphere; $37,500+ fine per violation
recovery_requirements.recovery_machine: Required; capture refrigerant into recovery cylinder before opening system
recovery_requirements.reclaim_vs_recycle: Reclaim: send to reprocessor; recycle: clean on-site for reuse in same system

common_leak_locations: Evaporator coil (pinhole leaks from formicary corrosion)

charging_methods.weigh_in_method: Most accurate; recover all refrigerant; weigh in nameplate charge ±2%
charging_methods.never_charge_liquid_to_suction: Destroys compressor; charge liquid to liquid line only; vapor to suction

gas_furnace_combustion.incomplete_combustion: Insufficient air; produces CO (carbon monoxide); deadly; adjust air shutter
gas_furnace_combustion.combustion_analyzer: Measures O₂, CO, CO₂, efficiency, draft; required for proper adjustment

natural_gas.o2: 5-9% oxygen in flue gas; <5% incomplete combustion; >9% excess air (heat loss)
natural_gas.co: <100 PPM air-free; <50 PPM ideal; >100 PPM indicates problem

draft.natural_draft: -0.02 to -0.04" WC in flue; negative = pulling air up chimney
draft.spillage_test: Hold match near draft hood; flame should pull in; if blows out, spillage (dangerous)

flame_appearance.good_flame: Blue with orange tips; stable; minimal yellow; lazy rolling flame
flame_appearance.yellow_flame: Incomplete combustion; high CO; soot; adjust air shutter or clean burners
flame_appearance.lifting_flame: Flame lifts off burner; too much air; reduce air shutter
flame_appearance.delayed_ignition: Flame rolls out; dirty burners or heat exchanger cracks; clean or replace
